data.content:
This is an **Android tombstone** crash log from a .NET MAUI app using the **Mono runtime** (`libmonosgen-2.0.so`). Let me analyze the frames and attempt symbolication.

**Crash summary:**
- **App**: `com.companyname.MauiTest` (arm64)
- **Signal**: SIGABRT — abort message: `"not compatible"`
- **Memory near x14**: `"some intentional crash on startup."`

The .NET runtime frames are **#01–#07** (all in `libmonosgen-2.0.so`, Build ID `1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be`). Frame #08 is JIT-compiled managed code. Let me try to fetch debug symbols from the Microsoft symbol server.

arguments.command: which addr2line llvm-symbolizer llvm-addr2line 2>/dev/null; echo "---"; dpkg -l | grep -i llvm 2>/dev/null | head -5

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.command:
# Try Microsoft symbol server - ELF debug info format
BUILDID="1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be"
# Try the standard symstore format
curl -sfSL -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so/${BUILDID}/libmonosgen-2.0.so" 2>&1
echo ""
# Try the _.debug format for ELF
curl -sfSL -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/_.debug" 2>&1
echo ""
# Try debuginfod
curl -sfSL -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/_.debug" 2>&1
echo ""
arguments.description: Probe symbol server for debug symbols

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Probe symbol server for debug symbols

data.reasoningText:
This is an Android tombstone/crash log (not an Apple .ips file), so the apple-crash-symbolication skill is not appropriate here. Let me analyze this crash log manually.

The crash is from a .NET MAUI app (`com.companyname.MauiTest`) on Android arm64. Key details:

- **Signal**: SIGABRT (signal 6)
- **Abort message**: 'not compatible'
- **Runtime**: Mono (`libmonosgen-2.0.so`)
- **Build ID**: `1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be`


The runtime frames are stacked in `libmonosgen-2.0.so` with frame #08 likely being JIT-compiled managed code, and frame #00 being the libc abort call. To symbolicate this properly, I'd need to locate the debug symbols for the Mono runtime using the Build ID, then apply `addr2line` or `llvm-symbolizer` to resolve the actual function names and line numbers.

For Android specifically, Microsoft publishes these symbols through their symbol server and as NuGet packages alongside the .NET MAUI workload. The symbols can be retrieved using the Build ID in the appropriate URL format on the Microsoft symbol server.

Let me try downloading the debug symbols using the Build ID `1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be` and see if I can symbolicate the addresses with available tools like `addr2line` or `llvm-symbolizer`.

arguments.command: which addr2line llvm-symbolizer llvm-addr2line 2>/dev/null; echo "---"; dpkg -l | grep -i llvm 2>/dev/null | head -5

# Try Microsoft symbol server - ELF debug info format
BUILDID="1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be"
# Try the standard symstore format
curl -sfSL -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so/${BUILDID}/libmonosgen-2.0.so" 2>&1
echo ""
# Try the _.debug format for ELF
curl -sfSL -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/_.debug" 2>&1
echo ""
# Try debuginfod
curl -sfSL -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/_.debug" 2>&1
echo ""
arguments.description: Probe symbol server for debug symbols

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "which addr2line llvm-symbolizer llvm-addr2line 2>/dev/null; echo \"---\"; dpkg -l | grep -i llvm 2>/dev/null | head -5", "description": "Check for symbolication tools", "initial_wait": 10}

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args:
{"command": "# Try Microsoft symbol server - ELF debug info format
BUILDID="1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be"
# Try the standard symstore format
curl -sfSL -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so/${BUILDID}/libmonosgen-2.0.so" 2>&1
echo ""
# Try the _.debug format for ELF
curl -sfSL -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/_.debug" 2>&1
echo ""
# Try debuginfod
curl -sfSL -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/_.debug" 2>&1
echo """, "description": "Probe symbol server for debug symbols", "initial_wait": 15}

toolArgs.command: which addr2line llvm-symbolizer llvm-addr2line 2>/dev/null; echo "---"; dpkg -l | grep -i llvm 2>/dev/null | head -5

arguments.command:
cd /tmp/sv-8a67d7ede08e4d94b287771a3d3befbe
# Symbolicate all .NET runtime frames using llvm-addr2line
echo "=== Symbolicating .NET Runtime Frames (libmonosgen-2.0.so) ==="
echo ""
for frame in "01 0x222098" "02 0x2241ac" "03 0x1abc80" "04 0x1abd14" "05 0x16dfa4" "06 0x16db6c" "07 0x16de04"; do
  num=$(echo $frame | cut -d' ' -f1)
  addr=$(echo $frame | cut -d' ' -f2)
  result=$(llvm-addr2line -f -e libmonosgen-2.0.so.debug $addr 2>&1)
  func=$(echo "$result" | head -1)
  loc=$(echo "$result" | tail -1)
  printf "#%s  pc %-12s  %s\n                         %s\n\n" "$num" "$addr" "$func" "$loc"
done

data.content:
Here's the full symbolicated backtrace:

```
#00  libc.so            abort
#01  libmonosgen-2.0.so ves_icall_System_Environment_FailFast          (icall.c:6244)
#02  libmonosgen-2.0.so ves_icall_System_Environment_FailFast_raw      (icall-def.h:202)
#03  libmonosgen-2.0.so do_icall                                       (interp.c:2457)
#04  libmonosgen-2.0.so get_interp_to_native_trampoline                (interp.c:1732)
#05  libmonosgen-2.0.so handle_isinst                                  (type-checking.c:0)
#06  libmonosgen-2.0.so handle_isinst                                  (type-checking.c:506)
#07  libmonosgen-2.0.so handle_isinst                                  (type-checking.c:528)
#08  <anonymous JIT>    (managed code — not symbolicated)
```

**Analysis:**

- The app called **`Environment.FailFast()`** with the message `"not compatible"`, which triggered `SIGABRT`.
- The call originated from **managed (JIT) code** (frame #08) running through the Mono **interpreter trampoline** (`do_icall` → `interp.c`).
- Frames #05–#07 show **`handle_isinst`** (type-checking / `is`/`as` cast logic in `type-checking.c`), suggesting a type compatibility check preceded the `FailFast` call.
- The UTF-16 string near x14 confirms the intent: *"some intentional crash on startup."*

**In short**: This is a deliberate crash — the app detected an incompatibility at startup and called `Environment.FailFast("not compatible")`. Frame #08 (anonymous JIT code) would require the app's managed symbols (`.pdb` / AOT symbols) to resolve further.
